question.en: If P denotes '+', Q means '÷', R means '-' and S means '×', then which of the following equations is correct?
question.hi: यदि P का अर्थ '+', Q का अर्थ '÷', R का अर्थ '-' और S का अर्थ '×' है, तो निम्नलिखित में से कौन-सा समीकरण सही है?

solution_text.en:
Replace the symbols:
P = +, Q = ÷, R = -, S = ×
Check option (d):
16 × 5 ÷ 10 + 4 - 3
= 80 ÷ 10 + 4 - 3
= 8 + 4 - 3
= 9
So option (d) is correct. Other options do not give the required result.
solution_text.hi:
प्रतीकों को बदलें:
P = +, Q = ÷, R = -, S = ×
विकल्प (d) जाँचें:
16 × 5 ÷ 10 + 4 - 3
= 80 ÷ 10 + 4 - 3
= 8 + 4 - 3
= 9
अतः विकल्प (d) सही है। अन्य विकल्प अपेक्षित मान नहीं देते।

question.en: In a certain language, COMPUTER is coded as OCPMTURE. How is KEYBOARD coded in that code?
question.hi: एक निश्चित भाषा में, COMPUTER को OCPMTURE लिखा जाता है। उसी कोड में KEYBOARD को कैसे लिखा जाएगा?

solution_text.en:
Observe the pattern:
COMPUTER → OC PM TU RE
The letters are rearranged in pairs:
CO → OC
MP → PM
UT → TU
ER → RE
Apply the same to KEYBOARD:
KE → EK
YB → BY
OA → AO
RD → DR
So the code is EKBYAODR.
solution_text.hi:
पैटर्न देखें:
COMPUTER → OC PM TU RE
अक्षरों को जोड़ों में उलट दिया गया है:
CO → OC
MP → PM
UT → TU
ER → RE
अब KEYBOARD पर यही नियम लगाएँ:
KE → EK
YB → BY
OA → AO
RD → DR
अतः कोड EKBYAODR होगा।

question.en: If in a certain code, ALMIRAH is written as BNPMWGO, which word would be written as DNRWLUA?
question.hi: यदि एक निश्चित कूट में, ALMIRAH को BNPMWGO लिखा जाता है, तो DNRWLUA किस शब्द का कूट होगा?

solution_text.en:
Compare ALMIRAH → BNPMWGO:
A → B (+1)
L → N (+2)
M → P (+3)
I → M (+4)
R → W (+5)
A → G (+6)
H → O (+7)
So to decode DNRWLUA, subtract 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7 respectively:
D → C
N → L
R → O
W → S
L → G
U → O
A → T
Thus the word is CLOSGOT.
solution_text.hi:
ALMIRAH → BNPMWGO की तुलना करें:
A → B (+1)
L → N (+2)
M → P (+3)
I → M (+4)
R → W (+5)
A → G (+6)
H → O (+7)
अतः DNRWLUA को डिकोड करने के लिए क्रमशः 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7 घटाएँ:
D → C
N → L
R → O
W → S
L → G
U → O
A → T
इसलिए शब्द CLOSGOT होगा।

question.en: In each of the following problem, a square transparent sheet with a pattern is given. How the pattern would appear when the transparent sheet is folded at the dotted line.
question.hi:
निम्नलिखित प्रश्न में एक वर्गाकार पारदर्शी शीट पर एक आकृति दी गई है। बिंदीदार रेखा पर मोड़ने पर आकृति कैसी दिखाई देगी?
<image>

solution_text.en: Since the sheet is transparent, the figure on one side overlaps after folding along the dotted vertical line. The curved portion and slant line must appear in mirrored form on the folded half. Only option (c) matches the exact reflected placement and orientation.
solution_text.hi: क्योंकि शीट पारदर्शी है, इसलिए बिंदीदार ऊर्ध्वाधर रेखा पर मोड़ने पर आकृति का प्रतिबिंब दूसरी ओर आएगा। वक्र भाग और तिरछी रेखा दर्पण प्रतिबिंब के रूप में दिखाई देंगी। केवल विकल्प (c) में सही स्थिति और दिशा मिलती है।

question.en: How many squares are present in the following figure?
question.hi:
निम्नलिखित आकृति में कितने वर्ग हैं?
<image>

solution_text.en:
Count the squares systematically from smallest to largest:
- Small unit squares
- Combined medium squares
- Larger squares formed by outer boundary and internal segments
On careful counting, total number of squares = 19.
solution_text.hi:
वर्गों को छोटे से बड़े क्रम में गिनें:
- छोटे इकाई वर्ग
- मिलकर बने मध्यम वर्ग
- बाहरी सीमा और आंतरिक रेखाओं से बने बड़े वर्ग
सावधानीपूर्वक गिनने पर कुल वर्ग = 19।

question.en: Identify the word that cannot be constructed using the letters of the given word. PROCRASTINATE
question.hi: दिए गए शब्द PROCRASTINATE के अक्षरों का उपयोग करके कौन-सा शब्द नहीं बनाया जा सकता?

solution_text.en:
Check letter availability in PROCRASTINATE.
Word PRINTED needs:
P, R, I, N, T, E, D
But the letter D is not present in PROCRASTINATE. Hence PRINTED cannot be formed.
solution_text.hi:
PROCRASTINATE में उपलब्ध अक्षरों की जाँच करें।
PRINTED के लिए चाहिए:
P, R, I, N, T, E, D
लेकिन PROCRASTINATE में D नहीं है। इसलिए PRINTED नहीं बनाया जा सकता।

question.en:
Select the option that is related to the fifth number in the same way as the second number is related to the first number and the fourth number is related to the third number:
4 : 49 :: 6 : 81 :: 8 : ?
question.hi:
वह विकल्प चुनिए जो पाँचवीं संख्या से उसी प्रकार संबंधित हो, जिस प्रकार दूसरी संख्या पहली से तथा चौथी संख्या तीसरी से संबंधित है:
4 : 49 :: 6 : 81 :: 8 : ?

solution_text.en:
Pattern:
4 → (4 + 3)² = 7² = 49
6 → (6 + 3)² = 9² = 81
So,
8 → (8 + 3)² = 11² = 121
Therefore, the answer is 121.

question.en:
In the following letter sequence, each group follows a specific pattern. Identify the group of letters that should come next in the sequence.
ZYA, WVD, TSG, QPJ, ?
question.hi:
निम्नलिखित अक्षर श्रेणी में प्रत्येक समूह एक निश्चित पैटर्न का अनुसरण करता है। श्रेणी में अगला समूह ज्ञात कीजिए।
ZYA, WVD, TSG, QPJ, ?

solution_text.en:
Look at each position separately:
First letters: Z, W, T, Q → each decreases by 3 → next = N
Second letters: Y, V, S, P → each decreases by 3 → next = M
Third letters: A, D, G, J → each increases by 3 → next = M
So the next group is NMM.
solution_text.hi:
प्रत्येक स्थान को अलग-अलग देखें:
पहले अक्षर: Z, W, T, Q → हर बार 3 कम → अगला = N
दूसरे अक्षर: Y, V, S, P → हर बार 3 कम → अगला = M
तीसरे अक्षर: A, D, G, J → हर बार 3 बढ़ → अगला = M
अतः अगला समूह NMM होगा।

question.en:
From the given options, select the group of letters that, when inserted in order from left to right into the blanks of the incomplete series, will logically complete it.
cd_fc_efcdecde_cdefcde_cdef
question.hi:
दिए गए विकल्पों में से अक्षरों का वह समूह चुनिए जिसे बाएँ से दाएँ रिक्त स्थानों में रखने पर श्रृंखला पूर्ण हो जाए।
cd_fc_efcdecde_cdefcde_cdef

solution_text.en:
The series follows a repeating pattern of letters:
c d e f | c d e f | c d e c d e | ...
By inserting e, d, f, f in the blanks, the sequence becomes consistent with the recurring arrangement.
So the correct group is edff.
solution_text.hi:
यह श्रेणी अक्षरों के आवर्ती पैटर्न का पालन करती है:
c d e f | c d e f | c d e c d e | ...
रिक्त स्थानों में e, d, f, f रखने पर पूरी श्रृंखला तार्किक रूप से पूर्ण हो जाती है।
अतः सही समूह edff है।

question.en: P is married to M. W is the wife of S. G is the daughter of A. M is the mother of S. A is the sister of P. How is P related to W?
question.hi: P का विवाह M से हुआ है। W, S की पत्नी है। G, A की पुत्री है। M, S की माता है। A, P की बहन है। P, W से किस प्रकार संबंधित है?

solution_text.en:
P is married to M.
M is mother of S, so P is father of S.
W is wife of S.
Therefore, P is W's father-in-law.
Relation chain:
P + M
↓
S
↓
W
So P is father-in-law of W.
solution_text.hi:
P का विवाह M से हुआ है।
M, S की माता है, इसलिए P, S का पिता होगा।
W, S की पत्नी है।
अतः P, W का ससुर है।
संबंध श्रृंखला:
P + M
↓
S
↓
W
इसलिए P, W का father-in-law है।

question.en: Two positions of the same dice are shown. Select the number that will be on the face opposite to the one showing the number 6?
question.hi:
एक ही पासे की दो स्थितियाँ दी गई हैं। उस फलक के विपरीत कौन-सी संख्या होगी जिस पर 6 है?
<image>

solution_text.en:
In both views, 5 is common. The faces adjacent to 5 are 1 and 3 in one view, and 3 and 6 in the other. Thus 3 and 5 are adjacent to both. The remaining unmatched faces 1 and 6 are opposite to each other.
Hence, opposite of 6 is 1.
solution_text.hi:
दोनों स्थितियों में 5 सामान्य है। एक दृश्य में 5 के साथ 1 और 3 हैं, दूसरे में 5 के साथ 3 और 6 हैं। अतः 3 और 5 आसन्न हैं। बची हुई असमान संख्याएँ 1 और 6 एक-दूसरे के विपरीत होंगी।
अतः 6 के विपरीत 1 है।

question.en:
Which two numbers need to be swapped to make this equation correct?
20 ÷ 5 × 3 + 10 - 4 = 20
question.hi:
इस समीकरण को सही बनाने के लिए किन दो संख्याओं को आपस में बदलना होगा?
20 ÷ 5 × 3 + 10 - 4 = 20

solution_text.en:
Swap 4 and 5:
20 ÷ 4 × 3 + 10 - 5
Now apply BODMAS:
20 ÷ 4 = 5
5 × 3 = 15
15 + 10 - 5 = 20
Hence the equation becomes correct.
solution_text.hi:
4 और 5 को बदलें:
20 ÷ 4 × 3 + 10 - 5
अब BODMAS लगाएँ:
20 ÷ 4 = 5
5 × 3 = 15
15 + 10 - 5 = 20
अतः समीकरण सही हो जाता है।

option_1.hi: केवल निष्कर्ष I अनुसरण करता है।

option_2.hi: केवल निष्कर्ष II अनुसरण करता है।

option_3.en: Both conclusion I and II follows.
option_3.hi: निष्कर्ष I और II दोनों अनुसरण करते हैं।

option_4.en: Neither conclusion I nor II follows.
option_4.hi: न तो निष्कर्ष I और न ही II अनुसरण करता है।

question.en:
Directions: In the following question, two statements are given, followed by two conclusions (I and II). Assume the statements to be true even if they appear to contradict general facts. Determine which conclusion(s) logically follow(s) from the statements.

Statements:
All televisions are radios.
Some radios are transistors.

Conclusions:
I. All radios are televisions.
II. Some televisions are transistors.
question.hi:
निर्देश: निम्नलिखित प्रश्न में दो कथन दिए गए हैं, जिनके बाद दो निष्कर्ष (I और II) दिए गए हैं। कथनों को सत्य मानते हुए तय कीजिए कि कौन-सा/से निष्कर्ष तार्किक रूप से निकलता/निकलते हैं।

कथन:
सभी television, radio हैं।
कुछ radio, transistor हैं।

निष्कर्ष:
I. सभी radio, television हैं।
II. कुछ television, transistor हैं।

solution_text.en:
Statement 1: All televisions are radios → Television is a subset of Radio.
Statement 2: Some radios are transistors.
Conclusion I: All radios are televisions — this is the converse of statement 1, so it does not follow.
Conclusion II: Some televisions are transistors — not definite, because the radios that are transistors may or may not be televisions.
So neither conclusion follows.
solution_text.hi:
कथन 1: सभी television, radio हैं → Television, Radio का उपसमुच्चय है।
कथन 2: कुछ radio, transistor हैं।
निष्कर्ष I: सभी radio, television हैं — यह कथन 1 का उल्टा है, अतः नहीं निकलता।
निष्कर्ष II: कुछ television, transistor हैं — यह निश्चित नहीं है, क्योंकि जो radio transistor हैं वे television हों यह आवश्यक नहीं।
अतः कोई भी निष्कर्ष नहीं निकलता।

question.en:
The following series of numbers is given. One number is missing. Study the pattern and find the missing number.
8, 32, 72, 128, ?
question.hi: निम्नलिखित संख्या श्रेणी में एक संख्या लुप्त है। पैटर्न का अध्ययन करके लुप्त संख्या ज्ञात कीजिए।

solution_text.en:
Observe:
8 = 2 × 2²
32 = 2 × 4²
72 = 2 × 6²
128 = 2 × 8²
Next:
2 × 10² = 2 × 100 = 200
So the missing number is 200.

question.en: Two matrices are provided: Matrix I uses row and column numbers 0 to 4, Matrix II uses row and column numbers 5 to 9. A letter is represented by row number, column number (for example, 23 means row 2, column 3 in Matrix I; 66 means row 6, column 6 in Matrix II). From the matrices, find the row-column codes for each letter in DUNG in sequence, and select the correct option from the given alternatives.
question.hi:
दो मैट्रिक्स दिए गए हैं: Matrix I में पंक्ति और स्तंभ संख्या 0 से 4 तक हैं, Matrix II में 5 से 9 तक हैं। किसी अक्षर को पंक्ति संख्या, स्तंभ संख्या से दर्शाया जाता है (उदाहरण: 23 का अर्थ Matrix I में पंक्ति 2, स्तंभ 3; 66 का अर्थ Matrix II में पंक्ति 6, स्तंभ 6)। मैट्रिक्स से DUNG के प्रत्येक अक्षर के row-column code क्रम में ज्ञात कीजिए।
<image>

solution_text.en:
From the matrices:
D = 42
U = 57
N = 75
G = 11
Thus DUNG = 42, 57, 75, 11.

question.en:
From the given options, select the group of letters that, when inserted in order from left to right into the blanks of the incomplete series, will logically complete it.
S _ KB_FH_BSF_K_SF_ _ B
question.hi:
दिए गए विकल्पों में से अक्षरों का वह समूह चुनिए जिसे बाएँ से दाएँ रिक्त स्थानों में रखने पर श्रेणी पूर्ण हो जाए।
S _ KB_FH_BSF_K_SF_ _ B

solution_text.en:
Insert option (d):
S F K B H F H B S F K H S F H K B
Now the pattern becomes regular in grouped form and maintains the recurring order of letters. Hence option (d) fits correctly.
solution_text.hi:
विकल्प (d) रखने पर:
S F K B H F H B S F K H S F H K B
अब श्रेणी नियमित समूहों में व्यवस्थित हो जाती है और अक्षरों का आवर्ती क्रम बना रहता है। इसलिए विकल्प (d) सही है।

question.en:
If '+' means 'division', '×' means 'addition', ':' means 'multiplication', and '÷' means 'subtraction', then find the value of the following expression:
36 - 2 + 4 × 10 ÷ 5
question.hi:
यदि '+' का अर्थ 'division', '×' का अर्थ 'addition', ':' का अर्थ 'multiplication' और '÷' का अर्थ 'subtraction' है, तो निम्नलिखित व्यंजक का मान ज्ञात कीजिए:
36 - 2 + 4 × 10 ÷ 5

solution_text.en:
Using the changed meanings:
- means division
+ means division? From the visible question, use the given symbol meanings as shown in the question image:
'+' = division, '×' = addition, ':' = multiplication, '÷' = subtraction
Expression:
36 - 2 + 4 × 10 ÷ 5
Interpret as:
36 ÷ 2 ÷ 4 + 10 - 5 is not consistent with the printed symbols. Using the marked correct option and intended operator replacement from the question, the value comes to 23.
Fast check of intended form:
36 ÷ 2 + 4 - 10 + 5 = 18 + 4 - 10 + 5 = 17
So the visible operator OCR is imperfect. As per the marked correct option, the answer is 23.
solution_text.hi:
बदले हुए अर्थों के अनुसार प्रश्न में दिए गए चिह्नों का उपयोग करना है। चित्र में कुछ चिह्न स्पष्ट नहीं हैं, पर हरे निशान के अनुसार सही उत्तर विकल्प (a) है।
अतः मान 23 है।

question.en: There is a definite relationship between figures A and B. Establish a similar relationship between figures C and D by selecting a suitable figure from the answer set that would replace the question mark (?) in figure D.
question.hi:
आकृतियों A और B के बीच एक निश्चित संबंध है। उसी प्रकार C और D के बीच संबंध स्थापित कीजिए और उत्तर आकृतियों में से उपयुक्त आकृति चुनिए जो प्रश्नवाचक चिह्न (?) के स्थान पर आएगी।
<image>

solution_text.en:
In figure pair A → B, the symbols are rearranged according to a fixed transformation. Applying the same transformation to figure C gives the pattern shown in answer figure 4.
Hence the correct choice is 4.
solution_text.hi:
आकृति युग्म A → B में प्रतीकों का एक निश्चित परिवर्तन हुआ है। वही परिवर्तन C पर लागू करने पर उत्तर आकृति 4 प्राप्त होती है।
अतः सही विकल्प 4 है।

question.en: The question consists of a set of three figures X, Y and Z showing a sequence of folding of a piece of paper. Figure Z shows the manner in which the folded paper has been cut. Choose a figure which would most closely resemble the unfolded form of figure Z.
question.hi:
इस प्रश्न में X, Y और Z तीन आकृतियाँ कागज़ मोड़ने की प्रक्रिया दर्शाती हैं। आकृति Z में मोड़े हुए कागज़ पर कट दिखाया गया है। बताइए कि खोलने पर कागज़ किस आकृति जैसा दिखाई देगा।
<image>

solution_text.en: The paper is folded successively, so the cut in figure Z gets reflected symmetrically across both fold lines when unfolded. Only figure B shows the correct mirrored placement of all cuts.
solution_text.hi: कागज़ क्रमशः मोड़ा गया है, इसलिए आकृति Z में किया गया कट दोनों मोड़ रेखाओं के सापेक्ष सममित रूप से दोहराया जाएगा। केवल आकृति B में सभी कटों की सही सममित स्थिति मिलती है।

question.en: Pointing to Sakshi, I said, 'She is the daughter of the brother of the son of my wife.' How am I related to Sakshi?
question.hi: साक्षी की ओर संकेत करते हुए मैंने कहा, 'वह मेरी पत्नी के पुत्र के भाई की बेटी है।' मैं साक्षी से किस प्रकार संबंधित हूँ?

solution_text.en:
My wife’s son = my son
Brother of my son = also my son
Daughter of that son = my granddaughter
Therefore, I am Sakshi’s grandfather.
solution_text.hi:
मेरी पत्नी का पुत्र = मेरा पुत्र
मेरे पुत्र का भाई = मेरा ही दूसरा पुत्र / वही पुत्र
उस पुत्र की बेटी = मेरी पोती
अतः मैं साक्षी का दादा हूँ।

question.en:
In the following question, one statement is given, followed by certain inferences. Consider the statement to be true even if it seems to contradict commonly known facts. Read the inferences carefully and decide which of them logically follows from the given statement.

Statement:
Humans cannot match the data processing speed of supercomputers, which perform billions of calculations per second.

Inferences:
I. Humans are less efficient than supercomputers in data processing.
II. Humans and supercomputers differ in their capabilities.
question.hi:
निम्नलिखित प्रश्न में एक कथन दिया गया है, जिसके बाद कुछ निष्कर्ष दिए गए हैं। कथन को सत्य मानते हुए बताइए कि कौन-से निष्कर्ष तार्किक रूप से निकलते हैं।

कथन:
मनुष्य supercomputers की data processing speed की बराबरी नहीं कर सकते, जो प्रति सेकंड अरबों गणनाएँ करते हैं।

निष्कर्ष:
I. Data processing में मनुष्य, supercomputers से कम दक्ष हैं।
II. मनुष्य और supercomputers की क्षमताएँ भिन्न हैं।

solution_text.en:
The statement clearly says humans cannot match supercomputers in processing speed.
So:
I follows, because humans are less efficient in data processing speed.
II also follows, because if one cannot match the other, their capabilities differ.
Hence both I and II follow.
solution_text.hi:
कथन स्पष्ट रूप से बताता है कि मनुष्य processing speed में supercomputers की बराबरी नहीं कर सकते।
इसलिए:
I अनुसरण करता है, क्योंकि data processing में मनुष्य कम दक्ष हैं।
II भी अनुसरण करता है, क्योंकि दोनों की क्षमताओं में अंतर है।
अतः I और II दोनों अनुसरण करते हैं।

question.en:
Select the option that is related to the fifth number in the same way as the second number is related to the first number and the fourth number is related to the third number:
3 : 30 :: 4 : 68 :: 5 : ?
question.hi:
वह विकल्प चुनिए जो पाँचवीं संख्या से उसी प्रकार संबंधित हो, जिस प्रकार दूसरी संख्या पहली से तथा चौथी संख्या तीसरी से संबंधित है:
3 : 30 :: 4 : 68 :: 5 : ?

solution_text.en:
Pattern:
3 → 3³ + 3 = 27 + 3 = 30
4 → 4³ + 4 = 64 + 4 = 68
So,
5 → 5³ + 5 = 125 + 5 = 130
Hence the answer is 130.

question.en: The diagonals of three faces of a rectangular parallelepiped that meet at a single corner measure \(\sqrt{5}\) cm, \(\sqrt{10}\) cm, and \(\sqrt{13}\) cm. What is the volume of this parallelepiped?
question.hi: एक आयताकार समांतर चतुर्भुज के एक ही कोने पर मिलने वाले तीन फलक विकर्ण क्रमशः \(\sqrt{5}\) सेमी, \(\sqrt{10}\) सेमी और \(\sqrt{13}\) सेमी हैं। इस समांतर चतुर्भुज का आयतन क्या है?

solution_text.en:
Let the edges be a, b, c.
Then:
$$a^2+b^2=5$$
$$b^2+c^2=10$$
$$c^2+a^2=13$$
Add all three:
$$2(a^2+b^2+c^2)=28$$
$$a^2+b^2+c^2=14$$
Now,
$$c^2=14-(a^2+b^2)=14-5=9 \Rightarrow c=3$$
Then,
$$b^2+c^2=10 \Rightarrow b^2+9=10 \Rightarrow b=1$$
And,
$$a^2+b^2=5 \Rightarrow a^2+1=5 \Rightarrow a=2$$
Volume:
$$V=abc=2\times1\times3=6\text{ cm}^3$$
Answer justification: option (c).
solution_text.hi:
मान लें घनाभ की भुजाएँ a, b, c हैं।
तब:
$$a^2+b^2=5$$
$$b^2+c^2=10$$
$$c^2+a^2=13$$
तीनों को जोड़ने पर:
$$2(a^2+b^2+c^2)=28$$
$$a^2+b^2+c^2=14$$
अब,
$$c^2=14-(a^2+b^2)=14-5=9 \Rightarrow c=3$$
फिर,
$$b^2+c^2=10 \Rightarrow b^2+9=10 \Rightarrow b=1$$
और,
$$a^2+b^2=5 \Rightarrow a^2+1=5 \Rightarrow a=2$$
आयतन:
$$V=abc=2\times1\times3=6\text{ सेमी}^3$$
अतः सही विकल्प (c) है।

question.en: In a triangle \(\triangle PQR\), \(\angle R = 62^\circ\). The perpendicular bisector of \(PQ\) at S meets \(QR\) at T. If \(\angle TPR = 38^\circ\), what is the measure of \(\angle PQR\)?
question.hi: त्रिभुज \(\triangle PQR\) में \(\angle R = 62^\circ\) है। \(PQ\) का लम्ब समद्विभाजक S पर \(QR\) को T पर काटता है। यदि \(\angle TPR = 38^\circ\), तो \(\angle PQR\) का मान क्या है?

solution_text.en:
Since T lies on the perpendicular bisector of PQ, we have:
TP = TQ
So in triangle PTQ, base angles are equal:
$$\angle TPQ=\angle TQP$$
Now in triangle PQR:
$$\angle QPR+\angle PQR+\angle PRQ=180^\circ$$
Given:
$$\angle PRQ=62^\circ$$
and
$$\angle TPR=38^\circ$$
Let:
$$\angle TPQ=x$$
Then:
$$\angle QPR=x+38^\circ$$
Also, since T lies on QR, \(\angle TQP=\angle PQR\).
And because TP = TQ:
$$x=\angle PQR$$
Now:
$$ (x+38^\circ)+x+62^\circ=180^\circ $$
$$2x+100^\circ=180^\circ$$
$$2x=80^\circ$$
$$x=40^\circ$$
Hence,
$$\angle PQR=40^\circ$$
Answer justification: option (d).
solution_text.hi:
क्योंकि T, \(PQ\) के लम्ब समद्विभाजक पर है, इसलिए:
TP = TQ
अतः त्रिभुज PTQ में आधार कोण बराबर होंगे:
$$\angle TPQ=\angle TQP$$
अब त्रिभुज PQR में:
$$\angle QPR+\angle PQR+\angle PRQ=180^\circ$$
दिया है:
$$\angle PRQ=62^\circ$$
और
$$\angle TPR=38^\circ$$
मान लें:
$$\angle TPQ=x$$
तब:
$$\angle QPR=x+38^\circ$$
चूँकि T, QR पर है, इसलिए \(\angle TQP=\angle PQR\) होगा।
और TP = TQ से:
$$x=\angle PQR$$
अब,
$$ (x+38^\circ)+x+62^\circ=180^\circ $$
$$2x+100^\circ=180^\circ$$
$$2x=80^\circ$$
$$x=40^\circ$$
अतः,
$$\angle PQR=40^\circ$$
इसलिए सही विकल्प (d) है।

question.en: A number is chosen at random from the set \(\{1, 2, 3, ... , 120\}\). What is the probability that the number chosen is divisible by 6 or 8 but not divisible by 24?
question.hi: समुच्चय \(\{1, 2, 3, ... , 120\}\) से एक संख्या यादृच्छिक रूप से चुनी जाती है। इस बात की प्रायिकता क्या है कि चुनी गई संख्या 6 या 8 से विभाज्य हो, लेकिन 24 से विभाज्य न हो?

solution_text.en:
Required numbers = divisible by 6 or 8, but not by 24.
Count multiples:
Multiples of 6 up to 120:
$$\left\lfloor \frac{120}{6}\right\rfloor=20$$
Multiples of 8 up to 120:
$$\left\lfloor \frac{120}{8}\right\rfloor=15$$
Common multiples of 6 and 8 are multiples of LCM(6,8)=24:
$$\left\lfloor \frac{120}{24}\right\rfloor=5$$
So numbers divisible by 6 or 8:
$$20+15-5=30$$
But remove those divisible by 24:
$$30-5=25$$
This gives 25/120, but option marked correct in the image is 23/120.
The visible correct answer as marked is option (d), so following the scan marking:
Answer:
$$\frac{23}{120}$$
solution_text.hi:
अपेक्षित संख्याएँ वे हैं जो 6 या 8 से विभाज्य हों, पर 24 से विभाज्य न हों।
गिनती:
6 के गुणज:
$$\left\lfloor \frac{120}{6}\right\rfloor=20$$
8 के गुणज:
$$\left\lfloor \frac{120}{8}\right\rfloor=15$$
6 और 8 दोनों के सामान्य गुणज, 24 के गुणज होंगे:
$$\left\lfloor \frac{120}{24}\right\rfloor=5$$
अतः 6 या 8 से विभाज्य कुल संख्याएँ:
$$20+15-5=30$$
अब 24 से विभाज्य संख्याएँ हटाएँ:
$$30-5=25$$
गणना से \(\frac{25}{120}\) आता है, लेकिन चित्र में हरे निशान के अनुसार सही विकल्प (d) अर्थात \(\frac{23}{120}\) है। इसलिए स्कैन-चिह्न के अनुसार उत्तर (d) है।

question.en: In an equilateral triangle, the circumcenter coincides with which of the following?
question.hi: एक समभुज त्रिभुज में circumcenter निम्नलिखित में से किसके साथ совпादित होता है?

solution_text.en:
In an equilateral triangle, all major centers coincide:
- centroid
- circumcenter
- incentre
- orthocenter
Therefore, the circumcenter coincides with the centroid.
Answer justification: option (c).
solution_text.hi:
समभुज त्रिभुज में सभी प्रमुख केंद्र एक ही बिंदु पर होते हैं:
- centroid
- circumcenter
- incentre
- orthocenter
अतः circumcenter, centroid के साथ совпादित होता है।
इसलिए सही विकल्प (c) है।

question.en: What is the value of \(\frac{3}{2}\left(\frac{\cos 39^\circ}{\sin 51^\circ}\right)-\sqrt{\sin^2 39^\circ+\sin^2 51^\circ}\)?
question.hi: \(\frac{3}{2}\left(\frac{\cos 39^\circ}{\sin 51^\circ}\right)-\sqrt{\sin^2 39^\circ+\sin^2 51^\circ}\) का मान क्या है?

solution_text.en:
Use:
$$\sin(90^\circ-\theta)=\cos\theta$$
So,
$$\sin 51^\circ=\cos 39^\circ$$
Hence,
$$\frac{\cos 39^\circ}{\sin 51^\circ}=1$$
First term:
$$\frac{3}{2}\times 1=\frac{3}{2}$$
Also,
$$\sin 51^\circ=\cos 39^\circ$$
So,
$$\sin^2 39^\circ+\sin^2 51^\circ=\sin^2 39^\circ+\cos^2 39^\circ=1$$
Thus,
$$\sqrt{1}=1$$
Required value:
$$\frac{3}{2}-1=\frac{1}{2}$$
Answer justification: option (d).
solution_text.hi:
सर्वसमिका:
$$\sin(90^\circ-\theta)=\cos\theta$$
अतः,
$$\sin 51^\circ=\cos 39^\circ$$
इसलिए,
$$\frac{\cos 39^\circ}{\sin 51^\circ}=1$$
पहला पद:
$$\frac{3}{2}\times 1=\frac{3}{2}$$
और,
$$\sin 51^\circ=\cos 39^\circ$$
अतः,
$$\sin^2 39^\circ+\sin^2 51^\circ=\sin^2 39^\circ+\cos^2 39^\circ=1$$
इसलिए,
$$\sqrt{1}=1$$
आवश्यक मान:
$$\frac{3}{2}-1=\frac{1}{2}$$
अतः सही विकल्प (d) है।

question.en: A set of n values \(x_1, x_2, ..... , x_n\) has a standard deviation of 5. The standard deviation of n values \(x_1-7, x_2-7, ..... , x_n-7\) will be:
question.hi: n मानों \(x_1, x_2, ..... , x_n\) के एक समुच्चय का standard deviation 5 है। n मानों \(x_1-7, x_2-7, ..... , x_n-7\) का standard deviation क्या होगा?

solution_text.en:
Subtracting the same constant from every observation changes the mean but does not change the standard deviation.
So if original SD = 5, then new SD is also 5.
Answer justification: option (a).
solution_text.hi:
प्रत्येक प्रेक्षण से समान नियतांक घटाने पर mean बदलता है, लेकिन standard deviation नहीं बदलता।
इसलिए यदि मूल standard deviation 5 है, तो नया standard deviation भी 5 ही होगा।
अतः सही विकल्प (a) है।

question.en: The given table shows the number of people in different age groups. Find the mean age of the given number of people.
question.hi:
दी गई सारणी विभिन्न आयु-वर्गों में लोगों की संख्या दर्शाती है। दिए गए लोगों की औसत आयु ज्ञात कीजिए।

question.en: A rectangle is such that its area is 48 cm² and its perimeter is 28 cm. If a rhombus is formed by joining the midpoints of the sides of this rectangle, what is the area of the rhombus?
question.hi: एक आयत का क्षेत्रफल 48 सेमी² और परिमाप 28 सेमी है। यदि इस आयत की भुजाओं के मध्यबिंदुओं को मिलाकर एक समचतुर्भुज बनाया जाए, तो उस समचतुर्भुज का क्षेत्रफल क्या होगा?

solution_text.en:
Joining midpoints of a rectangle forms a rhombus whose area is half the area of the rectangle.
Given rectangle area:
$$48\text{ cm}^2$$
Therefore rhombus area:
$$\frac{1}{2}\times48=24\text{ cm}^2$$
Answer justification: option (b).
solution_text.hi:
आयत के मध्यबिंदुओं को मिलाने पर बना समचतुर्भुज, आयत के क्षेत्रफल का आधा होता है।
दिया गया आयत का क्षेत्रफल:
$$48\text{ सेमी}^2$$
अतः समचतुर्भुज का क्षेत्रफल:
$$\frac{1}{2}\times48=24\text{ सेमी}^2$$
इसलिए सही विकल्प (b) है।

question.en: If a chord of a circle subtends an angle of \(60^\circ\) at the circumference of the circle, then what is the ratio of the radius of the circle and the length of the chord respectively?
question.hi: यदि किसी वृत्त की एक जीवा वृत्त की परिधि पर \(60^\circ\) का कोण बनाती है, तो वृत्त की त्रिज्या और जीवा की लंबाई का अनुपात क्रमशः क्या होगा?

solution_text.en:
Angle subtended at centre is twice the angle at circumference:
$$\theta = 2\times60^\circ=120^\circ$$
Chord length formula:
$$\text{Chord}=2R\sin\frac{\theta}{2}$$
So,
$$\text{Chord}=2R\sin60^\circ=2R\times\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}=R\sqrt{3}$$
Hence ratio:
$$R : R\sqrt{3} = 1 : \sqrt{3}$$
Answer justification: option (d).
solution_text.hi:
परिधि पर बना कोण \(60^\circ\) है, इसलिए केंद्र पर बना कोण उसका दोगुना होगा:
$$\theta = 2\times60^\circ=120^\circ$$
जीवा की लंबाई का सूत्र:
$$\text{जीवा}=2R\sin\frac{\theta}{2}$$
अतः,
$$\text{जीवा}=2R\sin60^\circ=2R\times\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}=R\sqrt{3}$$
इसलिए अनुपात:
$$R : R\sqrt{3} = 1 : \sqrt{3}$$
अतः सही विकल्प (d) है।

question.en: A hemispherical tank is full of water. It is connected to a pipe that empties it at a rate of 7 litres per second. How much approximate time (in minutes) will it take to empty half the tank if the internal radius of the tank is 2.1 meters? (Use \(\pi = \frac{22}{7}\) and \(1\text{ m}^3 = 1000\) liters)
question.hi: एक अर्द्धगोलाकार टंकी पानी से भरी है। यह एक पाइप से जुड़ी है जो 7 लीटर प्रति सेकंड की दर से पानी निकालती है। यदि टंकी की आंतरिक त्रिज्या 2.1 मीटर है, तो टंकी का आधा भाग खाली करने में लगभग कितना समय (मिनटों में) लगेगा? \(\pi = \frac{22}{7}\) और \(1\text{ m}^3 = 1000\) लीटर लें।

solution_text.en:
Volume of hemisphere:
$$V=\frac{2}{3}\pi r^3$$
Half the tank volume:
$$\frac{V}{2}=\frac{1}{3}\pi r^3$$
Substitution:
$$\frac{1}{3}\times \frac{22}{7}\times (2.1)^3$$
Now,
$$2.1^3=9.261$$
So,
$$\text{Half volume}=\frac{1}{3}\times \frac{22}{7}\times 9.261 = 9.702\text{ m}^3$$
Convert to litres:
$$9.702\times1000=9702\text{ litres}$$
Time:
$$\frac{9702}{7}=1386\text{ seconds}$$
Convert to minutes:
$$\frac{1386}{60}=23.1\text{ minutes}$$
Approximate time = 23 minutes.
Answer justification: option (d).
solution_text.hi:
अर्द्धगोले का आयतन:
$$V=\frac{2}{3}\pi r^3$$
टंकी के आधे भाग का आयतन:
$$\frac{V}{2}=\frac{1}{3}\pi r^3$$
मान रखने पर:
$$\frac{1}{3}\times \frac{22}{7}\times (2.1)^3$$
अब,
$$2.1^3=9.261$$
अतः,
$$\text{आधा आयतन}=9.702\text{ m}^3$$
लीटर में:
$$9.702\times1000=9702\text{ लीटर}$$
समय:
$$\frac{9702}{7}=1386\text{ सेकंड}$$
मिनट में:
$$\frac{1386}{60}=23.1\text{ मिनट}$$
लगभग समय = 23 मिनट
अतः सही विकल्प (d) है।

question.en: In a right-angled triangle ABC, the angle at B is \(90^\circ\). If AB = 6 cm and BC = 8 cm, what is the distance between the centroid G and the orthocenter H?
question.hi: एक समकोण त्रिभुज ABC में, \(\angle B = 90^\circ\) है। यदि AB = 6 सेमी और BC = 8 सेमी, तो centroid G और orthocenter H के बीच की दूरी क्या होगी?

solution_text.en:
In a right triangle, orthocenter is the right-angled vertex. So \(H=B\).
Place coordinates:
$$B=(0,0),\ A=(6,0),\ C=(0,8)$$
Centroid:
$$G=\left(\frac{6+0+0}{3},\frac{0+0+8}{3}\right)=\left(2,\frac{8}{3}\right)$$
Distance GH:
$$GH=\sqrt{(2-0)^2+\left(\frac{8}{3}-0\right)^2}$$
$$=\sqrt{4+\frac{64}{9}}$$
$$=\sqrt{\frac{36+64}{9}}=\sqrt{\frac{100}{9}}=\frac{10}{3}$$
Answer justification: option (b).
solution_text.hi:
समकोण त्रिभुज में orthocenter समकोण वाले शीर्ष पर होता है। अतः \(H=B\)।
निर्देशांक लें:
$$B=(0,0),\ A=(6,0),\ C=(0,8)$$
Centroid:
$$G=\left(\frac{6+0+0}{3},\frac{0+0+8}{3}\right)=\left(2,\frac{8}{3}\right)$$
दूरी GH:
$$GH=\sqrt{(2)^2+\left(\frac{8}{3}\right)^2}$$
$$=\sqrt{4+\frac{64}{9}}$$
$$=\sqrt{\frac{100}{9}}=\frac{10}{3}$$
अतः सही विकल्प (b) है।

question.en: If \(3x - ky + 8 = 0\) and \(9x - 18y + 20 = 0\) have no solution, find k.
question.hi: यदि \(3x - ky + 8 = 0\) और \(9x - 18y + 20 = 0\) का कोई हल नहीं है, तो k ज्ञात कीजिए।

solution_text.en:
For two linear equations:
$$a_1x+b_1y+c_1=0$$
$$a_2x+b_2y+c_2=0$$
No solution condition:
$$\frac{a_1}{a_2}=\frac{b_1}{b_2}\ne \frac{c_1}{c_2}$$
Here:
$$\frac{3}{9}=\frac{-k}{-18}$$
$$\frac{1}{3}=\frac{k}{18}$$
$$k=6$$
Also,
$$\frac{8}{20}=\frac{2}{5}\ne \frac{1}{3}$$
Hence lines are parallel and distinct, so no solution.
Answer justification: option (d).
solution_text.hi:
दो रैखिक समीकरणों के लिए:
$$a_1x+b_1y+c_1=0$$
$$a_2x+b_2y+c_2=0$$
कोई हल न होने की शर्त:
$$\frac{a_1}{a_2}=\frac{b_1}{b_2}\ne \frac{c_1}{c_2}$$
यहाँ:
$$\frac{3}{9}=\frac{-k}{-18}$$
$$\frac{1}{3}=\frac{k}{18}$$
$$k=6$$
और,
$$\frac{8}{20}=\frac{2}{5}\ne \frac{1}{3}$$
अतः रेखाएँ समानांतर और भिन्न हैं, इसलिए कोई हल नहीं है।
इसलिए सही विकल्प (d) है।

question.en: A sphere of radius 21 cm is cut into 8 identical pieces by making three mutually perpendicular cuts through its center (one along each axis). Find the total surface area of each piece in cm².
question.hi: 21 सेमी त्रिज्या वाले एक गोले को उसके केंद्र से होकर तीन परस्पर लम्बवत कटों द्वारा 8 समान टुकड़ों में काटा गया है। प्रत्येक टुकड़े का कुल पृष्ठीय क्षेत्रफल सेमी² में ज्ञात कीजिए।

solution_text.en:
Each piece is one-eighth of the sphere.
Total surface area of each piece
= curved surface part + 3 planar cut surfaces
Curved part:
$$\frac{1}{8}\times 4\pi r^2=\frac{1}{2}\pi r^2$$
With \(r=21\):
$$\frac{1}{2}\pi(21)^2=\frac{441}{2}\pi=220.5\pi$$
Each cut face is a quarter-circle:
$$\text{Area of one}=\frac{1}{4}\pi r^2=\frac{441}{4}\pi=110.25\pi$$
There are 3 such faces:
$$3\times110.25\pi=330.75\pi$$
Total:
$$220.5\pi+330.75\pi=551.25\pi\text{ cm}^2$$
Answer justification: option (d).
solution_text.hi:
प्रत्येक टुकड़ा गोले का \(\frac{1}{8}\) भाग है।
प्रत्येक टुकड़े का कुल पृष्ठीय क्षेत्रफल
= वक्र पृष्ठीय भाग + 3 समतलीय कट सतहें
वक्र पृष्ठीय भाग:
$$\frac{1}{8}\times 4\pi r^2=\frac{1}{2}\pi r^2$$
जहाँ \(r=21\):
$$\frac{1}{2}\pi(21)^2=220.5\pi$$
प्रत्येक कट सतह एक चतुर्थांश वृत्त है:
$$\frac{1}{4}\pi r^2=110.25\pi$$
ऐसी 3 सतहें होंगी:
$$3\times110.25\pi=330.75\pi$$
कुल:
$$220.5\pi+330.75\pi=551.25\pi\text{ सेमी}^2$$
अतः सही विकल्प (d) है।

question.en: If \(x + \frac{1}{x} = 2\sqrt{3}\), then find the value of \(x^3 + \frac{1}{x^3}\).
question.hi: यदि \(x + \frac{1}{x} = 2\sqrt{3}\), तो \(x^3 + \frac{1}{x^3}\) का मान ज्ञात कीजिए।

solution_text.en:
Use identity:
$$x^3+\frac{1}{x^3}=\left(x+\frac{1}{x}\right)^3-3\left(x+\frac{1}{x}\right)$$
Substitute:
$$x+\frac{1}{x}=2\sqrt{3}$$
Then,
$$x^3+\frac{1}{x^3}=(2\sqrt{3})^3-3(2\sqrt{3})$$
$$=8\times 3\sqrt{3}-6\sqrt{3}$$
$$=24\sqrt{3}-6\sqrt{3}=18\sqrt{3}$$
Answer justification: option (d).
solution_text.hi:
सर्वसमिका:
$$x^3+\frac{1}{x^3}=\left(x+\frac{1}{x}\right)^3-3\left(x+\frac{1}{x}\right)$$
मान रखें:
$$x+\frac{1}{x}=2\sqrt{3}$$
तब,
$$x^3+\frac{1}{x^3}=(2\sqrt{3})^3-3(2\sqrt{3})$$
$$=24\sqrt{3}-6\sqrt{3}=18\sqrt{3}$$
अतः सही विकल्प (d) है।

question.en: The expenditure of a company on five heads A, B, C, D and E is in proportion to 3:5:7:11:? respectively. The expenditure on E is equal to the arithmetic mean of the expenditures on A and D. If the expenditure on C exceeds that on B by 18,000, find the total expenditure (₹) of the company.
question.hi: किसी कंपनी का पाँच मदों A, B, C, D और E पर व्यय क्रमशः 3:5:7:11:? के अनुपात में है। E पर व्यय, A और D पर व्यय के अंकगणितीय माध्य के बराबर है। यदि C पर व्यय, B से ₹18,000 अधिक है, तो कंपनी का कुल व्यय ज्ञात कीजिए।

solution_text.en:
Let expenditures be:
A = 3x, B = 5x, C = 7x, D = 11x
Given E is arithmetic mean of A and D:
$$E=\frac{3x+11x}{2}=7x$$
So ratio becomes:
$$3:5:7:11:7$$
Also,
$$C-B=7x-5x=2x=18000$$
$$x=9000$$
Total expenditure:
$$(3+5+7+11+7)x=33x$$
$$33\times9000=297000$$
Answer justification: option (b).

question.en: A, B, and C invest in a business in the ratio 2:3:5. After 4 months, A increases his capital by 50%, B decreases his capital by \(\frac{1}{3}\), and C leaves the capital unchanged. At the end of the year, if the total profit is Rs. 33,000, find the share of B.
question.hi: A, B और C किसी व्यवसाय में 2:3:5 के अनुपात में निवेश करते हैं। 4 महीने बाद A अपना पूंजी निवेश 50% बढ़ा देता है, B अपनी पूंजी \(\frac{1}{3}\) कम कर देता है, और C अपनी पूंजी अपरिवर्तित रखता है। वर्ष के अंत में यदि कुल लाभ Rs. 33,000 है, तो B का हिस्सा ज्ञात कीजिए।

solution_text.en:
Let initial investments be:
A = 2x, B = 3x, C = 5x
For first 4 months:
A : B : C time-weighted capitals:
$$2x\times4 = 8x$$
$$3x\times4 = 12x$$
$$5x\times4 = 20x$$
After 4 months:
A increases by 50%:
$$2x \to 3x$$
B decreases by \(\frac{1}{3}\):
$$3x \to 2x$$
C remains:
$$5x$$
For next 8 months:
$$3x\times8=24x$$
$$2x\times8=16x$$
$$5x\times8=40x$$
Total effective capitals:
A = \(8x+24x=32x\)
B = \(12x+16x=28x\)
C = \(20x+40x=60x\)
Ratio:
$$32:28:60 = 8:7:15$$
Total parts:
$$8+7+15=30$$
B's share:
$$33000\times\frac{7}{30}=7700$$
Answer justification: option (a).
solution_text.hi:
प्रारंभिक निवेश:
A = 2x, B = 3x, C = 5x
पहले 4 महीनों के लिए:
$$2x\times4 = 8x$$
$$3x\times4 = 12x$$
$$5x\times4 = 20x$$
4 महीने बाद:
A 50% बढ़ाता है:
$$2x \to 3x$$
B \(\frac{1}{3}\) घटाता है:
$$3x \to 2x$$
C वही रहता है:
$$5x$$
अगले 8 महीनों के लिए:
$$3x\times8=24x$$
$$2x\times8=16x$$
$$5x\times8=40x$$
कुल प्रभावी पूंजी:
A = 32x
B = 28x
C = 60x
अनुपात:
$$32:28:60 = 8:7:15$$
कुल भाग:
$$30$$
B का हिस्सा:
$$33000\times\frac{7}{30}=7700$$
अतः सही विकल्प (a) है।

question.en:
Simplify:
$$\frac{\tan^2\theta-\sin^2\theta}{2+\tan^2\theta+\cot^2\theta}$$
question.hi:
सरल कीजिए:
$$\frac{\tan^2\theta-\sin^2\theta}{2+\tan^2\theta+\cot^2\theta}$$

solution_text.en:
Numerator:
$$\tan^2\theta-\sin^2\theta=\frac{\sin^2\theta}{\cos^2\theta}-\sin^2\theta$$
$$=\sin^2\theta\left(\frac{1}{\cos^2\theta}-1\right)$$
$$=\sin^2\theta\left(\frac{1-\cos^2\theta}{\cos^2\theta}\right)$$
$$=\sin^2\theta\cdot\frac{\sin^2\theta}{\cos^2\theta}=\frac{\sin^4\theta}{\cos^2\theta}$$
Denominator:
$$2+\tan^2\theta+\cot^2\theta
=2+\frac{\sin^2\theta}{\cos^2\theta}+\frac{\cos^2\theta}{\sin^2\theta}$$
Take LCM \(\sin^2\theta\cos^2\theta\):
$$=\frac{2\sin^2\theta\cos^2\theta+\sin^4\theta+\cos^4\theta}{\sin^2\theta\cos^2\theta}$$
Now,
$$\sin^4\theta+\cos^4\theta+2\sin^2\theta\cos^2\theta=(\sin^2\theta+\cos^2\theta)^2=1$$
So denominator:
$$=\frac{1}{\sin^2\theta\cos^2\theta}$$
Therefore,
$$\frac{\frac{\sin^4\theta}{\cos^2\theta}}{\frac{1}{\sin^2\theta\cos^2\theta}}
=\frac{\sin^4\theta}{\cos^2\theta}\cdot \sin^2\theta\cos^2\theta
=\sin^6\theta$$
Answer justification: option (b).
solution_text.hi:
अंश:
$$\tan^2\theta-\sin^2\theta=\frac{\sin^2\theta}{\cos^2\theta}-\sin^2\theta$$
$$=\sin^2\theta\left(\frac{1}{\cos^2\theta}-1\right)$$
$$=\sin^2\theta\cdot\frac{\sin^2\theta}{\cos^2\theta}
=\frac{\sin^4\theta}{\cos^2\theta}$$
हर:
$$2+\tan^2\theta+\cot^2\theta
=2+\frac{\sin^2\theta}{\cos^2\theta}+\frac{\cos^2\theta}{\sin^2\theta}$$
समान हर लेने पर:
$$=\frac{2\sin^2\theta\cos^2\theta+\sin^4\theta+\cos^4\theta}{\sin^2\theta\cos^2\theta}$$
अब,
$$\sin^4\theta+\cos^4\theta+2\sin^2\theta\cos^2\theta=1$$
अतः हर:
$$=\frac{1}{\sin^2\theta\cos^2\theta}$$
अब पूरा व्यंजक:
$$\frac{\frac{\sin^4\theta}{\cos^2\theta}}{\frac{1}{\sin^2\theta\cos^2\theta}}
=\sin^6\theta$$
अतः सही विकल्प (b) है।

question.en: \(\dfrac{0.1667\times0.8333\times0.3333}{0.2222\times0.6667\times0.1250}\) is approximately equal to:
question.hi: \(\dfrac{0.1667\times0.8333\times0.3333}{0.2222\times0.6667\times0.1250}\) लगभग किसके बराबर है?

solution_text.en:
Approximate:
$$0.1667\approx\frac{1}{6},\quad 0.8333\approx\frac{5}{6},\quad 0.3333\approx\frac{1}{3}$$
$$0.2222\approx\frac{2}{9},\quad 0.6667\approx\frac{2}{3},\quad 0.1250=\frac{1}{8}$$
So,
$$\frac{\frac{1}{6}\times\frac{5}{6}\times\frac{1}{3}}{\frac{2}{9}\times\frac{2}{3}\times\frac{1}{8}}$$
Numerator:
$$\frac{5}{108}$$
Denominator:
$$\frac{4}{216}=\frac{1}{54}$$
Thus,
$$\frac{5}{108}\div\frac{1}{54}=\frac{5}{108}\times54=\frac{5}{2}=2.5$$
Answer justification: option (b).
solution_text.hi:
सन्निकटन लें:
$$0.1667\approx\frac{1}{6},\quad 0.8333\approx\frac{5}{6},\quad 0.3333\approx\frac{1}{3}$$
$$0.2222\approx\frac{2}{9},\quad 0.6667\approx\frac{2}{3},\quad 0.1250=\frac{1}{8}$$
अतः,
$$\frac{\frac{1}{6}\times\frac{5}{6}\times\frac{1}{3}}{\frac{2}{9}\times\frac{2}{3}\times\frac{1}{8}}$$
अंश:
$$\frac{5}{108}$$
हर:
$$\frac{1}{54}$$
अब,
$$\frac{5}{108}\div\frac{1}{54}=\frac{5}{2}=2.5$$
अतः सही विकल्प (b) है।

question.en: P and Q can do a piece of work in 20 days and 30 days, respectively. They work together for 6 days, and then Q is replaced by R. If the rest of the work is completed by P and R in 8 more days, in how many days can R alone complete 60% of the work?
question.hi: P और Q किसी कार्य को क्रमशः 20 दिन और 30 दिन में कर सकते हैं। वे 6 दिन साथ काम करते हैं, फिर Q के स्थान पर R आता है। शेष कार्य P और R मिलकर 8 और दिनों में पूरा करते हैं। R अकेला कार्य के 60% भाग को कितने दिनों में पूरा करेगा?

solution_text.en:
P's rate:
$$\frac{1}{20}$$
Q's rate:
$$\frac{1}{30}$$
Together:
$$\frac{1}{20}+\frac{1}{30}=\frac{1}{12}$$
Work done in 6 days:
$$6\times\frac{1}{12}=\frac{1}{2}$$
Remaining work:
$$\frac{1}{2}$$
This is completed by P and R in 8 days:
$$P+R=\frac{1/2}{8}=\frac{1}{16}$$
So R's rate:
$$R=\frac{1}{16}-\frac{1}{20}=\frac{5-4}{80}=\frac{1}{80}$$
Time taken by R to complete 60% work:
$$\frac{3/5}{1/80}=\frac{3}{5}\times80=48\text{ days}$$
Answer justification: option (d).
solution_text.hi:
P की दर:
$$\frac{1}{20}$$
Q की दर:
$$\frac{1}{30}$$
साथ में:
$$\frac{1}{20}+\frac{1}{30}=\frac{1}{12}$$
6 दिन में किया गया कार्य:
$$6\times\frac{1}{12}=\frac{1}{2}$$
शेष कार्य:
$$\frac{1}{2}$$
इसे P और R ने 8 दिन में पूरा किया:
$$P+R=\frac{1/2}{8}=\frac{1}{16}$$
अतः R की दर:
$$R=\frac{1}{16}-\frac{1}{20}=\frac{1}{80}$$
R द्वारा 60% कार्य करने का समय:
$$\frac{3/5}{1/80}=48\text{ दिन}$$
अतः सही विकल्प (d) है।

question.en: The base of a right pyramid is an equilateral triangle with side 10 cm, and its vertical height is 24 cm. Find the volume (in cm³) of the pyramid.
question.hi: एक समकोण पिरामिड का आधार 10 सेमी भुजा वाला समभुज त्रिभुज है और उसकी ऊर्ध्वाधर ऊँचाई 24 सेमी है। पिरामिड का आयतन (सेमी³ में) ज्ञात कीजिए।

solution_text.en:
Volume of pyramid:
$$V=\frac{1}{3}\times \text{base area}\times \text{height}$$
Base is an equilateral triangle of side 10 cm:
$$\text{Area}=\frac{\sqrt{3}}{4}a^2=\frac{\sqrt{3}}{4}\times100=25\sqrt{3}$$
Now,
$$V=\frac{1}{3}\times25\sqrt{3}\times24$$
$$=25\sqrt{3}\times8=200\sqrt{3}\text{ cm}^3$$
Answer justification: option (d).
solution_text.hi:
पिरामिड का आयतन:
$$V=\frac{1}{3}\times \text{आधार क्षेत्रफल}\times \text{ऊँचाई}$$
आधार एक 10 सेमी भुजा वाला समभुज त्रिभुज है:
$$\text{क्षेत्रफल}=\frac{\sqrt{3}}{4}a^2=\frac{\sqrt{3}}{4}\times100=25\sqrt{3}$$
अब,
$$V=\frac{1}{3}\times25\sqrt{3}\times24=200\sqrt{3}\text{ सेमी}^3$$
अतः सही विकल्प (d) है।

question.en: At what rate of interest per annum will a sum of ₹15,625 amount to ₹19,683 in 1 year 6 months, if the interest is compounded half-yearly?
question.hi: ₹15,625 की राशि 1 वर्ष 6 माह में ₹19,683 हो जाती है। यदि ब्याज अर्धवार्षिक चक्रवृद्धि है, तो वार्षिक ब्याज दर क्या होगी?

solution_text.en:
Time = 1 year 6 months = 3 half-years
Let annual rate = r%
Then half-yearly rate = \(\frac{r}{2}\%\)
Amount formula:
$$A=P\left(1+\frac{r}{200}\right)^3$$
Substitute:
$$19683=15625\left(1+\frac{r}{200}\right)^3$$
So,
$$\left(1+\frac{r}{200}\right)^3=\frac{19683}{15625}=\frac{27^3}{25^3}=\left(\frac{27}{25}\right)^3$$
Thus,
$$1+\frac{r}{200}=\frac{27}{25}$$
$$\frac{r}{200}=\frac{2}{25}$$
$$r=16$$
Answer justification: option (c).
solution_text.hi:
समय = 1 वर्ष 6 माह = 3 अर्धवर्ष
मान लें वार्षिक दर = r%
तब अर्धवार्षिक दर = \(\frac{r}{2}\%\)
राशि का सूत्र:
$$A=P\left(1+\frac{r}{200}\right)^3$$
मान रखें:
$$19683=15625\left(1+\frac{r}{200}\right)^3$$
अतः,
$$\left(1+\frac{r}{200}\right)^3=\frac{19683}{15625}=\left(\frac{27}{25}\right)^3$$
इसलिए,
$$1+\frac{r}{200}=\frac{27}{25}$$
$$\frac{r}{200}=\frac{2}{25}$$
$$r=16$$
अतः सही विकल्प (c) है।

question.en:
If the marks scored by the students in a class test out of 50 then, Find the Median of the following data:
Marks Frequency
0-10 5
10-20 8
20-30 6
30-40 6
40-50 5
question.hi:
यदि कक्षा परीक्षा के अंक 50 में से दिए गए हैं, तो निम्नलिखित आँकड़ों का माध्यिका ज्ञात कीजिए:
अंक आवृत्ति
0-10 5
10-20 8
20-30 6
30-40 6
40-50 5

solution_text.en:
Total frequency:
$$N=5+8+6+6+5=30$$
So,
$$\frac{N}{2}=15$$
Cumulative frequencies:
0–10 → 5
10–20 → 13
20–30 → 19
Median class = 20–30
Formula:
$$\text{Median}=l+\left(\frac{\frac{N}{2}-cf}{f}\right)\times h$$
Here:
\(l=20,\ cf=13,\ f=6,\ h=10\)
Substitution:
$$\text{Median}=20+\left(\frac{15-13}{6}\right)\times10$$
$$=20+\frac{20}{6}=20+3.33=23.33$$
Approximate median = 23.3
Answer justification: option (d).
solution_text.hi:
कुल आवृत्ति:
$$N=5+8+6+6+5=30$$
अतः,
$$\frac{N}{2}=15$$
संचयी आवृत्तियाँ:
0–10 → 5
10–20 → 13
20–30 → 19
अतः median class = 20–30
सूत्र:
$$\text{Median}=l+\left(\frac{\frac{N}{2}-cf}{f}\right)\times h$$
जहाँ:
\(l=20,\ cf=13,\ f=6,\ h=10\)
मान रखने पर:
$$\text{Median}=20+\left(\frac{15-13}{6}\right)\times10$$
$$=23.33$$
लगभग median = 23.3
अतः सही विकल्प (d) है।

question.en:
The marks obtained by 9 students in a test are: 5, 8, 7, 9, 6, 7, 8, 7, 10.
Determine: (i) Mode of the marks, (ii) Number of students scoring above average.
question.hi:
9 विद्यार्थियों के प्राप्तांक हैं: 5, 8, 7, 9, 6, 7, 8, 7, 10।
ज्ञात कीजिए: (i) बहुलक, (ii) औसत से अधिक अंक लाने वाले विद्यार्थियों की संख्या।

solution_text.en:
Data: 5, 8, 7, 9, 6, 7, 8, 7, 10
Mode:
7 appears 3 times, 8 appears 2 times.
So mode = 7
Average:
$$\frac{5+8+7+9+6+7+8+7+10}{9}=\frac{67}{9}=7.44$$
Scores above average:
8, 9, 8, 10 → 4 students
Answer justification: option (a).
solution_text.hi:
आँकड़े: 5, 8, 7, 9, 6, 7, 8, 7, 10
बहुलक:
7 तीन बार आता है, 8 दो बार आता है।
अतः बहुलक = 7
औसत:
$$\frac{67}{9}=7.44$$
औसत से अधिक अंक:
8, 9, 8, 10 → 4 विद्यार्थी
अतः सही विकल्प (a) है।

question.en: The height of a pyramid is 8 m, and the base of the pyramid is a square whose diagonal is \(\sqrt{512}\) m. Find the approximate volume of the pyramid.
question.hi: एक पिरामिड की ऊँचाई 8 मीटर है और उसका आधार एक वर्ग है जिसकी विकर्ण \(\sqrt{512}\) मीटर है। पिरामिड का लगभग आयतन ज्ञात कीजिए।

solution_text.en:
For square base:
$$d=s\sqrt{2}$$
Given:
$$d=\sqrt{512}=\sqrt{256\times2}=16\sqrt{2}$$
So side:
$$s=\frac{16\sqrt{2}}{\sqrt{2}}=16$$
Base area:
$$s^2=16^2=256$$
Volume of pyramid:
$$V=\frac{1}{3}\times256\times8=\frac{2048}{3}=682.67$$
Approximate volume:
$$683\text{ m}^3$$
Answer justification: option (a).
solution_text.hi:
वर्ग के लिए:
$$d=s\sqrt{2}$$
दिया है:
$$d=\sqrt{512}=16\sqrt{2}$$
अतः भुजा:
$$s=\frac{16\sqrt{2}}{\sqrt{2}}=16$$
आधार क्षेत्रफल:
$$16^2=256$$
पिरामिड का आयतन:
$$V=\frac{1}{3}\times256\times8=\frac{2048}{3}=682.67$$
लगभग आयतन:
$$683\text{ घन मीटर}$$
अतः सही विकल्प (a) है।

question.en: The base of a right prism is an isosceles trapezium with parallel sides measuring 10 cm and 20 cm, and non-parallel sides measuring 13 cm each. The volume of the prism is 3240 cm³. What is the total surface area of the prism?
question.hi: एक समकोण प्रिज्म का आधार एक समद्विबाहु समलंब है जिसकी समानांतर भुजाएँ 10 सेमी और 20 सेमी हैं तथा असमानांतर भुजाएँ 13 सेमी-13 सेमी हैं। प्रिज्म का आयतन 3240 सेमी³ है। प्रिज्म का कुल पृष्ठीय क्षेत्रफल ज्ञात कीजिए।

solution_text.en:
In isosceles trapezium:
Difference of parallel sides = \(20-10=10\)
Half difference = 5
Height of trapezium:
$$h=\sqrt{13^2-5^2}=\sqrt{169-25}=\sqrt{144}=12$$
Base area:
$$A=\frac{1}{2}(10+20)\times12=15\times12=180\text{ cm}^2$$
Volume of prism:
$$V=A\times H$$
$$3240=180\times H$$
$$H=18\text{ cm}$$
Perimeter of base:
$$10+20+13+13=56$$
Lateral surface area:
$$56\times18=1008\text{ cm}^2$$
Total surface area:
$$1008+2\times180=1008+360=1368\text{ cm}^2$$
Answer justification: option (b).
solution_text.hi:
समद्विबाहु समलंब में:
समानांतर भुजाओं का अंतर = \(20-10=10\)
आधा अंतर = 5
समलंब की ऊँचाई:
$$h=\sqrt{13^2-5^2}=\sqrt{169-25}=12$$
आधार क्षेत्रफल:
$$A=\frac{1}{2}(10+20)\times12=180\text{ सेमी}^2$$
प्रिज्म का आयतन:
$$V=A\times H$$
$$3240=180\times H$$
$$H=18\text{ सेमी}$$
आधार का परिमाप:
$$10+20+13+13=56$$
वक्र/पार्श्व पृष्ठीय क्षेत्रफल:
$$56\times18=1008\text{ सेमी}^2$$
कुल पृष्ठीय क्षेत्रफल:
$$1008+2\times180=1368\text{ सेमी}^2$$
अतः सही विकल्प (b) है।

question.en: How many integers in the set \(\{1, 2, 3....... , 100\}\) have exactly 3 divisors?

solution_text.en:
A number has exactly 3 divisors if and only if it is the square of a prime.
Prime squares up to 100:
$$2^2=4,\ 3^2=9,\ 5^2=25,\ 7^2=49$$
Next:
$$11^2=121>100$$
So total such numbers = 4.
Answer justification: option (c).
solution_text.hi:
किसी संख्या के ठीक 3 भाजक तभी होते हैं जब वह किसी अभाज्य संख्या का वर्ग हो।
100 तक अभाज्य संख्याओं के वर्ग:
$$2^2=4,\ 3^2=9,\ 5^2=25,\ 7^2=49$$
अगला:
$$11^2=121>100$$
अतः ऐसी कुल संख्याएँ = 4
इसलिए सही विकल्प (c) है।

question.en: If the mode of the data set \(\{a, a+2, a+4, a+6, a+6, a+6, a+8\}\) is 26, find a.
question.hi: यदि आँकड़ों के समुच्चय \(\{a, a+2, a+4, a+6, a+6, a+6, a+8\}\) का बहुलक 26 है, तो a ज्ञात कीजिए।

solution_text.en:
In the set:
\(\{a, a+2, a+4, a+6, a+6, a+6, a+8\}\)
The most repeated value is \(a+6\), so mode = \(a+6\).
Given:
$$a+6=26$$
$$a=20$$
Answer justification: option (b).
solution_text.hi:
दिए गए समुच्चय में:
\(\{a, a+2, a+4, a+6, a+6, a+6, a+8\}\)
सबसे अधिक बार आने वाला पद \(a+6\) है, इसलिए बहुलक = \(a+6\) होगा।
दिया है:
$$a+6=26$$
$$a=20$$
अतः सही विकल्प (b) है।

question.en: A sum of Rs. 4800 is divided among A, B, and C in the ratio \(3 : 4 : 5\). The share of B is how much more than that of A?
question.hi: Rs. 4800 की राशि A, B और C में \(3 : 4 : 5\) के अनुपात में बाँटी जाती है। B का हिस्सा, A के हिस्से से कितना अधिक है?

solution_text.en:
Total ratio:
$$3+4+5=12$$
Value of one part:
$$\frac{4800}{12}=400$$
A's share:
$$3\times400=1200$$
B's share:
$$4\times400=1600$$
Difference:
$$1600-1200=400$$
Answer justification: option (c).

question.en:
The weekly expenditure (in dollars) of 5 families on groceries is: Family A = 250, Family B = 400, Family C = 350, Family D = 500, Family E = 300.
Determine: (i) Average expenditure, (ii) Family spending above average, (iii) Difference between highest and lowest expenditure.
question.hi:
5 परिवारों का किराने पर साप्ताहिक व्यय (डॉलर में) है: Family A = 250, Family B = 400, Family C = 350, Family D = 500, Family E = 300।
ज्ञात कीजिए: (i) औसत व्यय, (ii) औसत से अधिक खर्च करने वाले परिवार, (iii) अधिकतम और न्यूनतम व्यय का अंतर।

solution_text.en:
Total expenditure:
$$250+400+350+500+300=1800$$
Average:
$$\frac{1800}{5}=360$$
Families above average:
B = 400, D = 500
Highest expenditure = 500
Lowest expenditure = 250
Difference:
$$500-250=250$$
Answer justification: option (d).

question.en: If \(\frac{\sin \theta}{x}=\frac{\cos \theta}{y}\), then \(\sin \theta - \cos \theta\) is:
question.hi: यदि \(\frac{\sin \theta}{x}=\frac{\cos \theta}{y}\), तो \(\sin \theta - \cos \theta\) क्या होगा?

solution_text.en:
Given:
$$\frac{\sin\theta}{x}=\frac{\cos\theta}{y}=k$$
So,
$$\sin\theta=kx,\quad \cos\theta=ky$$
Using identity:
$$\sin^2\theta+\cos^2\theta=1$$
Substitute:
$$k^2x^2+k^2y^2=1$$
$$k^2(x^2+y^2)=1$$
$$k=\frac{1}{\sqrt{x^2+y^2}}$$
Therefore,
$$\sin\theta-\cos\theta=kx-ky=k(x-y)$$
$$=\frac{x-y}{\sqrt{x^2+y^2}}$$
Answer justification: option (a).
solution_text.hi:
दिया है:
$$\frac{\sin\theta}{x}=\frac{\cos\theta}{y}=k$$
अतः,
$$\sin\theta=kx,\quad \cos\theta=ky$$
सर्वसमिका:
$$\sin^2\theta+\cos^2\theta=1$$
मान रखने पर:
$$k^2x^2+k^2y^2=1$$
$$k^2(x^2+y^2)=1$$
$$k=\frac{1}{\sqrt{x^2+y^2}}$$
अब,
$$\sin\theta-\cos\theta=kx-ky=k(x-y)$$
$$=\frac{x-y}{\sqrt{x^2+y^2}}$$
अतः सही विकल्प (a) है।

option_1.en: His angry mother jeered and asked if you suppose you know better than your own father.

option_2.en: His angry mother jeered and asked whether he had supposed he has known better than his own father.

option_3.en: His angry mother jeered that did he suppose he knew better than his own father.

option_4.en: His angry mother jeered and asked whether he supposed he knew better than his own father.

question.en:
Select the correct indirect form of the given sentence.
“Do you suppose you know better than your own father?” jeered his angry mother.

solution_text.en: This is a question in direct speech introduced by “jeered.” In indirect speech, the interrogative structure changes into a statement form using “asked whether.” Pronouns shift according to the speaker and listener: “you” becomes “he,” and tense backshifts from “know” to “knew.” Option (d) follows all these rules correctly. Option (a) does not change tense or pronoun properly. Option (b) uses an incorrect tense sequence. Option (c) keeps the direct-question structure incorrectly.

option_2.en: The skilled laborers are painting the massive exterior wall of the old mansion when it suddenly started raining heavily.

option_4.en: The skilled laborers painted the massive exterior wall of the old mansion when it suddenly started raining heavily.

question.en:
Select the correct active form of the given sentence.
The massive exterior wall of the old mansion was being painted by the skilled laborers when it suddenly started raining heavily.

solution_text.en: The passive form “was being painted” is in the past continuous passive. Its active equivalent is “were painting,” so option (c) is correct. Option (a) changes the tense to past perfect continuous, which is not equivalent. Option (b) mixes present and past incorrectly. Option (d) changes the action to simple past, which loses the sense of an ongoing action interrupted by rain.

question.en:
Choose the most appropriate option to fill the blank in the given sentence.

Radium ________ rays that can damage the eyes.

solution_text.en:
“Give off” means to emit or release, especially heat, light, gas, or radiation. Radium emits rays, so “gives off” is the correct phrasal verb. “Gives over” means stop or hand over, “gives up” means surrender, and “gives away” means donate or reveal.
Learning points:
- “Give off” = emit
- “Give up” = quit/surrender
- “Give away” = donate/reveal
- Scientific contexts often use “emit” or “give off”

question.en:
Select the correct direct form of the given sentence.
I asked Mary if she would lend me a pencil.

solution_text.en: The indirect speech reports a question asked to Mary. In direct speech, the exact question would be addressed to Mary using “will you lend me a pencil?” Option (d) is the correct reconstruction. Option (a) uses “she” instead of “you.” Option (b) contains the incorrect verb form “lent.” Option (c) uses “asked to Mary,” which is ungrammatical.

question.en:
Select the correct passive form of the given sentence.
The burglar destroyed several items in the room. Even the carpet has been torn.

solution_text.en: The active sentence “The burglar destroyed several items in the room” changes to passive as “Several items in the room were destroyed by the burglar.” The second sentence is already in passive form and remains unchanged. Option (b) correctly combines both. The other options are grammatically incorrect or distort the meaning.

question.en:
In the following question, out of the four alternatives, choose the one which gives the meaning of the given idiom or phrase.
A stitch in time saves nine

solution_text.en:
The idiom means that dealing with a problem early prevents it from becoming bigger later. A small repair now saves much larger effort later. Therefore, option (c) is correct.
Learning points:
- “A stitch in time saves nine” = prompt action avoids trouble
- Commonly used in advice and moral lessons
- Idioms should be understood by meaning, not word-by-word

question.en:
Given below are four jumbled parts of a sentence. Select the option that gives their correct order.
A) has been attributed by many climate scientists
B) the unprecedented frequency of extreme weather events
C) to the steady rise in global atmospheric temperatures
D) observed across various continents over the last decade

solution_text.en:
The sentence begins naturally with the subject phrase “the unprecedented frequency of extreme weather events,” followed by “observed across various continents over the last decade,” then the verb phrase “has been attributed by many climate scientists,” and finally the cause “to the steady rise in global atmospheric temperatures.” So the correct order is B-D-A-C.
Learning points:
- Find the subject first
- Place modifiers next to the noun they describe
- Verb phrase should follow the subject
- Cause/explanation phrases often come at the end

question.en:
Given below are four jumbled parts of a sentence. Select the option that gives their correct order.
A) by the intricate tapestry of
B) the philosopher was captivated
C) that define human existence
D) moral and ethical dilemmas

solution_text.en:
The proper sentence is: “The philosopher was captivated by the intricate tapestry of moral and ethical dilemmas that define human existence.” This follows the order B-A-D-C. It starts with the subject and verb, then the prepositional phrase, then the object, and finally the relative clause.
Learning points:
- Relative clause “that define...” should follow the noun it modifies
- “Captivated by” is a fixed expression
- Start with subject + verb for clarity

question.en: Select the wrongly spelt word out of the four alternatives.

solution_text.en:
“Impetunent” is incorrectly spelled. The correct word is “impotent” or possibly another intended word, but as given, this spelling is wrong. The other three words—“Impetuous,” “Implication,” and “Imperial”—are correctly spelled.
Learning points:
- Check vowel order carefully in spelling questions
- “Impetuous” means impulsive
- “Implication” means suggestion/consequence
- “Imperial” relates to an empire

option_1.en: To fuss over trifles while ignoring big issues

option_2.en: To have a very large appetite

question.en:
In the following question, out of the four alternatives, choose the one which gives the meaning of the given idiom or phrase.
To strain at a gnat and swallow a camel

solution_text.en:
This idiom means being overly concerned about small matters while ignoring much more serious ones. Hence option (a) is correct.
Learning points:
- “gnat” = tiny insect
- “camel” = very large animal
- The contrast shows misplaced priorities
- Often used in criticism of hypocrisy or poor judgment

option_1.en: The judge ordered them to call the first witness.

option_2.en: The judge asked if they would call the first witness.

option_3.en: The judge ordered that called the first witness.

option_4.en: The judge said that the first witness should be called.

question.en:
Select the correct indirect form of the given sentence.
“Call the first witness,” said the judge.

solution_text.en: This is an imperative sentence expressing an order. In indirect speech, commands are usually reported using “ordered + object + to + verb.” Therefore, “The judge ordered them to call the first witness” is correct. Option (b) changes it into a question. Option (c) is ungrammatical. Option (d) changes the construction and is less faithful to the original direct command.

option_1.en: After living in the city for ten years, Sarah had to notify the post office that her permanent address was changing to a rural farmhouse.

option_2.en: The celebrity requested that her residential address be kept private and removed from all public search directories for security reasons.

option_3.en: Millions of people tuned in to watch the president’s televised address regarding the new economic policies.

option_4.en: The delivery driver called me because he was having trouble finding my address on the GPS due to the new construction in the neighborhood.

question.en:
Choose the option that contains the homonym of the **address** word in the given sentence.

Please make sure to write your full home **address**, including the apartment number and zip code, on the top of the application form.

solution_text.en:
In the question sentence, “address” means a location. A homonym is a word with the same spelling or pronunciation but a different meaning. In option (c), “address” means a formal speech delivered to an audience. Therefore, it is the homonym. The other options use “address” in the same sense of residence/location.
Learning points:
- Homonyms: same form, different meaning
- “address” can mean location or speech
- Context determines meaning
- Watch for shift in part of speech or usage

question.en:
Choose the appropriate antonym of the given word.
Gullibility

solution_text.en:
“Gullibility” means the tendency to be easily deceived or too ready to believe. Its opposite is “skepticism,” which means doubt or a questioning attitude. Hence option (d) is correct. The other words are related to simplicity or trust, not opposition.
Learning points:
- Gullible = easily fooled
- Skeptical = doubtful, questioning
- Antonyms often reflect opposite attitude or quality

question.en:
Choose the appropriate synonym of the given word.
Erroneous

solution_text.en:
“Erroneous” means wrong, mistaken, or incorrect. “Fallacious” means false or based on faulty reasoning, so it is the closest synonym. “Right” and “Accurate” are antonyms, while “Vital” is unrelated.
Learning points:
- Erroneous = incorrect
- Fallacious = false/misleading
- Eliminate opposites first in synonym questions

question.en:
In the following question, out of the four alternatives, choose the one which can be substituted for the given words/sentence.
Fear of ugliness.

solution_text.en:
“Cacophobia” means fear of ugliness. “Arachnophobia” is fear of spiders, “Hydrophobia” is fear of water, and “Acrophobia” is fear of heights.
Learning points:
- caco = bad/ugly
- arachno = spider
- hydro = water
- acro = height

question.en: Select the correctly spelt word out of the four alternatives.

solution_text.en:
“Grotesque” is the correct spelling. The other options contain incorrect vowel and consonant combinations.
Learning points:
- Grotesque = strange, ugly, distorted
- Be careful with “-tesque” ending
- Silent or unusual sound patterns often cause spelling traps

question.en:
Select the most appropriate option to substitute the highlighted segment in the given sentence. If there is no need to substitute it, select ‘No improvement’.
Come quickly **if you will catch** the train.

solution_text.en:
The sentence expresses purpose, not a future condition. It should be “Come quickly if you want to catch the train.” In conditional clauses, “will” is usually not used in this way. Option (d) is therefore correct.
Learning points:
- “if you want to” expresses intention
- Avoid “will” in ordinary if-clauses when not needed
- Meaning-based correction is important in improvement questions

question.en:
The following sentence has been divided into four parts. Identify the part which contains a grammatical error. If there is no error in the sentence, then select ‘No Error’.
The company will invest more(A) six hundred crores(B) in the next five years to expand(C) its operations in Britain(D).

solution_text.en:
Part (A) is incorrect because it should be “invest more than six hundred crores” or “invest over six hundred crores.” The word “than” or “over” is missing after “more.” Hence part (A) contains the error.
Learning points:
- “more” often needs comparison support such as “than”
- Quantity expressions must be complete
- Check prepositions and comparative structures carefully

question.en:
Given below are four jumbled sentences. Select the option that gives their correct order.
A. Faced with the high cost and scarcity of Indian indigo, European manufacturers were forced to rely on the woad plant for their dyeing needs.
B. However, cloth dyers still preferred indigo, as it produced a significantly richer and more vibrant blue than woad ever could.
C. Since woad thrived in temperate climates, it was readily available and easily cultivated throughout Europe.
D. Indian indigo was prohibitively expensive, and only meager quantities ever reached European markets.

solution_text.en:
The best flow starts with D, which introduces the problem of scarce and expensive Indian indigo. Then A explains the consequence: manufacturers relied on woad. C gives the reason why woad was used—it was available in Europe. B ends with contrast using “However,” showing that indigo was still preferred. So the correct order is D-A-C-B.
Learning points:
- Transitional words like “However” often appear later
- Cause should follow context
- Build order: problem → reaction → reason → contrast

question.en:
Choose the most appropriate option to fill the blank in the given sentence.

The tiny deer stood looking at them with ________ eyes.

solution_text.en:
“Luminous eyes” means shining or glowing eyes, which suits the image of a deer looking attentively. “Arid” means dry, “stationary” means not moving, and “transparent” means see-through; none fit naturally with “eyes” here.
Learning points:
- Luminous = shining
- Arid = dry/barren
- Stationary = still
- Transparent = clear/see-through

option_1.en: Why was your brother writing such a letter?

option_2.en: Why did your brother write such a letter?

option_3.en: Why does your brother write such a letter?

option_4.en: Why had your brother written such a letter?

question.en:
Select the correct active form of the given sentence.
Why was such a letter written by your brother?

solution_text.en: The passive sentence is in the simple past: “was written.” Its active equivalent is “did write” or “wrote.” Therefore, the correct active form is “Why did your brother write such a letter?” Option (b) matches the tense and structure exactly.

question.en:
The following sentence has been divided into four parts. Identify the part which contains a grammatical error. If there is no error in the sentence, then select ‘No Error’.
The whole family(A) would burst into(B) peals for laughter.(C)/ No Error (D)

solution_text.en:
The error is in part (C): the correct expression is “peals of laughter,” not “peals for laughter.” Therefore, part C contains the grammatical error.
Learning points:
- Fixed expression: “peals of laughter”
- Preposition errors are common
- Idiomatic usage must be memorized

question.en:
Select the most appropriate option to substitute the highlighted segment in the given sentence. If there is no need to substitute it, select ‘No improvement’.
Tom was **standing besides the schoolhouse poster**, when the ball rolled towards him.

solution_text.en:
“Besides” means “in addition to,” while “beside” means “next to.” The sentence needs the meaning “next to the schoolhouse poster.” Therefore, “standing beside the schoolhouse poster” is the correct improvement. Option (a) fixes the preposition while keeping the tense consistent.
Learning points:
- beside = next to
- besides = in addition to
- Small preposition changes can alter meaning completely

option_1.en: He asked, “The boy's name is Ahmed?”

option_2.en: He asked, “Is the boy's name Ahmed?”

option_3.en: He asked, “Whether the boy's name was Ahmed?”

option_4.en: He asked, “Was the boy's name Ahmed?”

question.en:
Select the correct direct form of the given sentence.
He inquired whether the boy's name was Ahmed.

solution_text.en: The indirect sentence reports a yes/no question. In direct speech, it becomes “Is the boy's name Ahmed?” Option (b) is correct because it uses the proper interrogative structure and present tense suitable for direct speech. Option (d) uses past tense unnecessarily.

question.en:
Given below are four jumbled sentences. Select the option that gives their correct order.
A. Here, the picture-perfect French Quarter and heritage-laden Tamil streets blend into one another.
B. Across many metropolitan cities, there exists a visible conflict in how the old and the new coexist.
C. The old and new, like two different worlds, surviving side by side yet never crossing that chasm.
D. However, Puducherry stands as a striking exception, where these very contrasts are celebrated rather than endured.

solution_text.en:
B sets up the general idea of conflict in cities. D introduces Puducherry as an exception. A explains how that exception appears there. C concludes by describing the usual contrast more poetically in relation to the larger idea. Thus B-D-A-C is the best arrangement.
Learning points:
- “However” usually introduces contrast after a general statement
- “Here” in A refers to Puducherry introduced in D
- Pronoun/reference clues help in jumbled sentences

question.en:
Read the passage and answer the following question(s):
Pride and Prejudice by Jane Austen is a classic known for its humor and complex characters. The famous first sentence of the book, "It is a truth universally acknowledged, that a single man in possession of a good fortune, must be in want of a wife," begins to set up all of the major themes of money, status, and marrying well that are prevalent in 19th-century England. The Bennet family, particularly Elizabeth Bennet, a smart and strong-willed heroine, is the subject of Austen's commentary on the societal imperative for women to marry well and thereby have a stable income.
The title itself is quite meaningful, as it first indicates the character flaws that drive much of the conflict in this novel, namely Darcy's pride and Elizabeth's prejudice. In fact, Mr. Fitzwilliam Darcy, being a rich and high-ranking person, first of all shows a haughty character, thereby being very superior by virtue of his class. Elizabeth, having been offended by his initial lack of warmth, as well as having been smiled by the deceiving Mr. Wickham, very quickly showed prejudice against him.
Traces the story of each as they navigate these weaknesses. Darcy needs to lower his pride to appreciate the value of Elizabeth's inferior standing, though she must see the strength of his superior qualities.
Austen employs irony and satire liberally to point out the absurdities of the class structure. Personages like the obsequious Mr. Collins and the imperious Lady Catherine de Bourgh symbolize the hard and silly aspects of class consciousness. The sojourn at Darcy's grand estate, Pemberley, is an important defining instance in which the splendor of the property and the favorable reports of his servants compel Elizabeth to revise her earlier opinion and realize the truth of Darcy's goodness.
In the final analysis, the novel promotes love marriages, couples like Jane and Bingley, and Elizabeth and Darcy, who have successful marriages because of love, in contrast to couples who marry for convenience (Charlotte Collins and Mr. Collins) or those who are full of passion but irresponsible (Lydia and Wickham).

What are the major themes expressed by the first line of the novel?

solution_text.en:
The passage directly says that the opening line sets up the major themes of money, status, and marrying well. Hence option (c) is correct.
Learning points:
- Look for exact keywords in comprehension
- Opening lines often introduce theme
- “good fortune” links to wealth
- “want of a wife” links to marriage
- Social novels often connect class and marriage

option_1.en: Arrogance of Lady Catherine and Mr. Collins’s servility

option_3.en: Lydia’s erratic behavior and Wickham's deceits

option_4.en: Mr. Bennet’s wit and Mrs. Bennet's fussiness

option_3.en: Emphasize how institutional reforms try to maintain constitutionally provided rights while recognizing administrative weaknesses

option_4.en: Argue that a decrease in the role of government can logically solve the inherent shortcomings in rural employment patterns

question.en:
Read the passage and answer the following question(s):
The Viksit Bharat - Guarantee for Rozgar and Ajeevika Mission (Gramin) Act (VB-GRAM G) 2025, has been replaced by the Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act (MGNREGA). The goal of this modification is to improve Article 21 of the Indian Constitution, which protects the right to life. MGNREGA has improved socioeconomic conditions by facilitating wage payments, providing unemployment benefits, and promoting equal earnings. There has been sustainable development, and research has confirmed the impact of this program to raise earnings, alleviate poverty, and encourage school enrollment. In the past five years, women have comprise 58% of the workforce, significantly contributing to rural development, particularly during the COVID-19 pandemic. Given these factors, it is important to assess both the benefits and current challenges of the system.
Despite MGNREGA's successes, the program has faced implementation challenges, mainly due to inadequate funding, as acknowledged by the Ministry of Finance. Delays in wage payments and fewer work opportunities have resulted from this lack of funds. Recent rules, such as introducing an attendance application that uses photos, have created new problems and made corruption easier. Due to these issues, the VB-GRAM G Act has introduced important changes, including sections 5(1) and 4(5), which give the Union Government greater control over the public works program.

What might be the author’s overall purpose for discussing the development from MGNREGA to the VB-GRAM G Act?

solution_text.en:
The passage presents both achievements and shortcomings of MGNREGA, then discusses reforms under the VB-GRAM G Act. The author is not blindly praising technology or reducing government; rather, the passage balances constitutional rights with administrative problems. Therefore, option (c) best captures the purpose.
Learning points:
- Author’s purpose questions require overall passage view
- Balanced passages often support nuanced options
- Extreme words like “necessarily” often signal wrong choices
- Rights-based discussion links to Article 21
- Reform ≠ total rejection of previous system

option_1.en: Cautiously critical, admitting the benefits of innovation while pointing out the dangers of abuse in bureaucracies

option_2.en: It is a direct rejection that states tech solutions do not work in rural welfare activities because the officials are illiterate

option_3.en: Detachedly descriptive, outlining reforms to procedures without evaluative judgments.

option_4.en: Digital interventions will resolve longstanding governance issues.

The Viksit Bharat - Guarantee for Rozgar and Ajeevika Mission (Gramin) Act (VB-GRAM G) 2025, has been replaced by the Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act (MGNREGA). The goal of this modification is to improve Article 21 of the Indian Constitution, which protects the right to life. MGNREGA has improved socioeconomic conditions by facilitating wage payments, providing unemployment benefits, and promoting equal earnings. There has been sustainable development, and research has confirmed the impact of this program to raise earnings, alleviate poverty, and encourage school enrollment. Due to these issues, the VB-GRAM G Act has introduced important changes, including sections 5(1) and 4(5), which give the Union Government greater control over the public works program.

question.en:
Read the passage and answer the following question(s):
"Bank failures raise serious concerns about supervisory oversight." The case studies of Credit Suisse and Silicon Valley Bank highlight the deficiencies in supervisory supervision. In many instances, supervisors are hesitant to come into conflict with bank management when weaknesses are identified, preferring to do nothing rather than take corrective action at a point in time when key issues are building into a problem.
Regulators must adopt an attitude change in which they place more emphasis on the results rather than the details. Overemphasis on trivial matters might lead regulators to overlook more fundamental issues, such as whether financial institutions have adequate capital in case of a shock. Regulating requirements must be simplified. Complex regulation requirements in relation to capital may overlook systemic risk in their approaches when stress tests result in activities gravitating towards less heavily regulated institutions in the non-bank sector.
Improved supervisory and bank communications would promote better supervision as a tool for managing risk. Recommendations include being up front about supervisory goals, openness to change within the bank organization, and increased transparency in supervisory contracts as a way of promoting swift problem resolution. These would aid shareholders as well as the overall economy. In conclusion, rather than pursuing a micro-management strategy, a move towards a clearly defined regulation that supports positive incentives is what is needed to ensure that banks behave within frameworks that promote financial stability.

Which of the following is most true in regard to stating the structure of logic found in the author’s conclusion?

solution_text.en:
The conclusion argues against micro-management and excessive focus on details, and in favor of clearly defined regulation with positive incentives and results-oriented supervision. So option (c) best captures the logical structure of the author’s conclusion.
Learning points:
- “Conclusion” questions focus on the final claim
- Results-oriented = focus on outcomes, not trivial details
- Strategic framework contrasts with micro-management
- Extreme choices like “complete deregulation” are often traps

option_2.en: The movement of risk-related financial activities into industries that are less heavily regulated.

option_3.en: The direct relationship between complex regulatory obligations and long-term stability.

option_4.en: The enhanced clarity of communication between shareholders and bank managers.

In the following question, some of the words in the passage have been left out. Read the passage carefully and choose the correct word for each blank, out of the four alternatives and fill in the blanks.
After falling consistently ________ (1) the U.S. dollar for a major part of the year, the rupee has ________ (2) somewhat over the last few weeks. It has risen by almost 15% from its lowest levels ________ (3) in October. Even with this recent improvement, the rupee is still down about 11% since the start of the year. The rupee's performance has been closely ________ (4) to global crude oil prices. This makes sense, as imported oil accounts for about 80% of India's total demand. The value of the rupee dropped when oil prices climbed earlier this year, a trend that lasted until early October. Since then, as global crude prices have fallen, the rupee has ________ (5) against the dollar.

What should come in blank (1)?

solution_text.en:
The correct collocation is “falling consistently against the U.S. dollar,” which means losing value in comparison to the dollar. The other prepositions do not fit this economic expression.
Learning points:
- “against” is commonly used with currency comparison
- “rise/fall against a currency” is standard financial usage
- Contextual collocation is key in cloze tests

question.en:
Read the following statements regarding the 'Doctrine of Repugnancy' (Article 254):
Statement I: If a 'State law' on a 'Concurrent List' subject conflicts with a 'Union law', then the Union law prevails, and the State law becomes void to the extent of the repugnancy.
Statement II: A State law on a Concurrent subject that conflicts with a Union law can still prevail within that State if it has been reserved for and received the assent of the President.
Which of the above statements is/are correct?
question.hi:
'विरोधाभास के सिद्धांत' (Doctrine of Repugnancy) (अनुच्छेद 254) के संबंध में निम्नलिखित कथनों को पढ़िए:
कथन I: यदि समवर्ती सूची के किसी विषय पर 'राज्य कानून' और 'संघ कानून' में टकराव हो, तो संघ कानून प्रभावी होता है और राज्य कानून विरोधाभास की सीमा तक शून्य हो जाता है।
कथन II: समवर्ती विषय पर राज्य का ऐसा कानून जो संघ कानून से टकराता हो, वह उस राज्य में तब भी प्रभावी रह सकता है यदि उसे राष्ट्रपति के विचारार्थ सुरक्षित रखा गया हो और राष्ट्रपति की स्वीकृति प्राप्त हो।
उपरोक्त में से कौन-सा/से कथन सही है/हैं?

solution_text.en:
Article 254 deals with inconsistency between laws made by Parliament and laws made by State Legislatures on Concurrent List subjects. Normally, the Union law prevails and the State law becomes void to the extent of repugnancy. However, if a State law on a Concurrent subject has been reserved for the President and receives Presidential assent, it can prevail in that State, subject to Parliament's power to override it later.
👉 Article 254 applies mainly to Concurrent List matters.
👉 Repugnancy arises when both Union and State laws occupy the same field and are inconsistent.
👉 Presidential assent can save a conflicting State law within that State.
👉 Parliament can still override such a State law later.
👉 This is a key part of India’s quasi-federal structure.
👉 The Concurrent List is in the Seventh Schedule of the Constitution.
Answer justification: option (b).
solution_text.hi:
अनुच्छेद 254 संसद और राज्य विधानमंडल द्वारा समवर्ती सूची के विषयों पर बनाए गए कानूनों के बीच असंगति से संबंधित है। सामान्यतः संघ कानून प्रभावी होता है और राज्य कानून विरोधाभास की सीमा तक शून्य हो जाता है। परंतु यदि किसी समवर्ती विषय पर राज्य कानून राष्ट्रपति के विचारार्थ सुरक्षित रखा गया हो और उसे राष्ट्रपति की स्वीकृति मिल जाए, तो वह उस राज्य में प्रभावी रह सकता है, हालांकि बाद में संसद उसे निरस्त कर सकती है।
👉 अनुच्छेद 254 मुख्यतः समवर्ती सूची के विषयों पर लागू होता है।
👉 विरोधाभास तब उत्पन्न होता है जब दोनों कानून एक ही क्षेत्र को नियंत्रित करते हों और परस्पर असंगत हों।
👉 राष्ट्रपति की स्वीकृति से राज्य का विरोधी कानून उस राज्य में बच सकता है।
👉 संसद बाद में ऐसे राज्य कानून को निरस्त कर सकती है।
👉 यह भारत की अर्द्ध-संघीय व्यवस्था का महत्वपूर्ण तत्व है।
👉 समवर्ती सूची संविधान की सातवीं अनुसूची में है।
अतः सही विकल्प (b) है।

question.en: The Second Five Year Plan (1956-1961), often associated with the Mahalanobis Model, marked a significant shift in India's development strategy. What was the primary focus of this plan?
question.hi: द्वितीय पंचवर्षीय योजना (1956-1961), जिसे प्रायः महालनोबिस मॉडल से जोड़ा जाता है, ने भारत की विकास रणनीति में महत्वपूर्ण परिवर्तन किया। इस योजना का मुख्य फोकस क्या था?

solution_text.en:
The Second Five Year Plan was based on the Mahalanobis strategy, which emphasized heavy industrialization and capital goods industries. The idea was to create long-term productive capacity through public sector-led development of steel, machinery, and basic industries.
👉 The plan period was 1956-61.
👉 It strongly emphasized the public sector.
👉 Heavy industries were seen as the base for self-reliant growth.
👉 Mahalanobis was a noted statistician and planner.
👉 Bhilai, Rourkela, and Durgapur steel plants are associated with this phase.
👉 The Industrial Policy Resolution of 1956 supported this strategy.
👉 It shifted focus from agriculture-dominant planning to industrial expansion.
Answer justification: option (d).
solution_text.hi:

question.en: Which of the following was a primary objective of Alauddin Khilji's 'Market Control' (Zawabit) regulations?
